        {
          "file": "atr-release.yml",
          "workflow_name": "ATR Release Process",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "apache_dist",
            "docker_hub",
            "github_releases"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_dispatch (manual with release_version and release_candidate inputs)",
          "auth_method": "OIDC (GitHub id-token for ATR), secrets.ASF_USERNAME for ATR upload, secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN for GitHub Release",
          "publish_actions": [
            "apache/tooling-actions/upload-to-atr@main",
            "apache/tooling-actions/release-on-atr@main",
            "softprops/action-gh-release@v1"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [],
          "summary": "This workflow publishes official Apache Solr MCP releases through the Apache Trusted Releases (ATR) platform. It uploads source tarballs and JAR distributions to Apache dist infrastructure (via ATR), creates GitHub Releases with downloadable artifacts, and triggers Docker image publishing. The workflow follows a three-phase process: compose (build artifacts), vote (manual voting period), and finish (publish to Apache mirrors and announce). Note: Workflow is marked as experimental and blocked pending prerequisites (automated signing, ATR onboarding).",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "build-and-publish.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Build and Publish",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "ci_infrastructure",
          "ecosystems": [
            "ghcr",
            "docker_hub"
          ],
          "trigger": "push to main branch, version tags (v*), manual workflow_dispatch",
          "auth_method": "GITHUB_TOKEN for GHCR, DOCKERHUB_USERNAME and DOCKERHUB_TOKEN secrets for Docker Hub",
          "publish_actions": [
            "docker/login-action@v3"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [
            "./gradlew jib -Djib.to.image=ghcr.io/${{ steps.repo.outputs.owner_lc }}/solr-mcp:$TAG",
            "./gradlew jib -Djib.to.image=${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}/solr-mcp:$TAG"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow builds Docker images for the Solr MCP Server project and publishes them to GHCR and Docker Hub. Despite the extensive comments describing it as a 'development CI/CD' workflow, the images are published to personal/organizational namespaces (ghcr.io/{owner}/solr-mcp and {username}/solr-mcp) on every push to main with 'latest' tags. The workflow header comments indicate this is for development builds and testing, distinguishing it from release-publish.yml which handles official ASF releases. The images appear to be used for CI/CD infrastructure and development testing rather than end-user consumption, as evidenced by the tagging strategy (VERSION-SHORT_SHA for main branch) and the explicit distinction from the official release workflow.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "nightly-build.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Nightly Build",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "snapshot_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "docker_hub",
            "github_releases"

          "trigger": "schedule (cron: '0 2 * * *') and workflow_dispatch",

          "publish_actions": [
            "softprops/action-gh-release@v1"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [
            "./gradlew jib"
          ],
          "summary": "Scheduled nightly build workflow that publishes snapshot artifacts to Docker Hub (apache/solr-mcp-nightly) and GitHub pre-releases. Runs daily at 2 AM UTC to provide unstable/preview builds for testing latest changes. Creates source tarballs with SHA512 checksums and Docker images tagged with date stamps. Includes cleanup logic to retain only the last 7 nightly builds.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "release-publish.yml",

            "./gradlew jib -Djib.to.image=apache/solr-mcp:${{ inputs.release_version }}",
            "./gradlew jib -Djib.to.image=ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/solr-mcp:${{ inputs.release_version }}",
            "./mcp-publisher publish"
          ],
          "summary": "Official Apache Solr MCP release workflow that publishes Docker images to Docker Hub (apache/solr-mcp) and GitHub Container Registry after ASF voting process completes. Also publishes server metadata to the Model Context Protocol Registry using mcp-publisher CLI with GitHub OIDC authentication. Triggered manually by Release Manager with release version and RC number inputs. Includes validation, multi-tag publishing (version, major, minor, latest), SBOM generation, and release announcement creation.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        }

          "trigger": "schedule (daily 2:00 AM UTC) and workflow_dispatch",

            "./gradlew publishPlugin --no-configuration-cache"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow publishes nightly builds of a JetBrains IntelliJ plugin to the JetBrains Marketplace nightly channel. It runs daily at 2:00 AM UTC, checks for new commits since the last nightly tag, generates a nightly version number (e.g., 253.18970-nightly.1), builds the plugin, and publishes it using Gradle's publishPlugin task with signing credentials. The workflow also creates a git tag for version tracking and uploads the built artifact to GitHub Actions storage.",

The pre-release is intended for PMC review and voting. The workflow explicitly states it does NOT publish to JetBrains Marketplace (that happens in a separate release.yml workflow when the pre-release is promoted).         {
          "file": "docker.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Build & publish docker images",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "ci_infrastructure",
          "ecosystems": [
            "docker_hub"
          ],
          "trigger": "push to master or release branches, pull_request to master",
          "auth_method": "DOCKERHUB_USER and DOCKERHUB_TOKEN secrets",
          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "supersetbot docker --push --preset ${{ matrix.build_preset }} --context \"$EVENT\" --context-ref \"$RELEASE\" $FORCE_LATEST --extra-flags \"--build-arg INCLUDE_CHROMIUM=false --tag $IMAGE_TAG\" $PLATFORM_ARG"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow builds Docker images for Apache Superset with multiple build presets (dev, lean, py310, py311, py312, websocket, dockerize). On push events to master or release branches, it builds multi-platform images (linux/arm64, linux/amd64) and pushes them to Docker Hub with tags like 'apache/superset:GHA-{preset}-{run_id}'. On pull requests, it only builds single-platform (linux/amd64) images and loads them locally without pushing. The images are tagged with GitHub Actions run IDs and are used for CI/CD testing purposes, not as release artifacts for end users. The workflow uses a custom 'supersetbot docker' command to handle the build and push operations.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },

          "file": "embedded-sdk-release.yml",

          "publish_commands": [
            "npm run ci:release"
          ],
          "summary": "Publishes the Superset Embedded SDK package to npm registry on pushes to master or release branches. Uses npm ci:release command with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN authentication. Includes a pre-check to verify NPM_TOKEN secret exists before attempting build/publish.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },

            "gcr"

            "docker push -a $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY"
          ],
          "summary": "This deprecated workflow builds Docker images for ephemeral test environments triggered by PR labels. It builds a CI image using supersetbot, tags it with the PR number, and pushes it to AWS ECR (superset-ci repository). The image is then deployed to ECS for testing purposes. These are CI infrastructure images for temporary test environments, not release artifacts for end users.",

          ],
          "summary": "CI infrastructure workflow that builds and pushes Docker images for PR preview environments using the 'superset-showtime' tool. Triggered on PR events (labeled, unlabeled, synchronize, closed) or manual dispatch. Only authorized maintainers with write/admin permissions can trigger deployments. The workflow uses Docker Hub for storing PR-specific preview images, which are CI infrastructure artifacts for testing/preview purposes, not release artifacts for end users. The 'showtime sync' command handles Docker image building and pushing to Docker Hub based on PR state.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []

          "workflow_name": "Docs Deployment",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "documentation",
          "ecosystems": [
            "github_pages"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_run on Python-Integration completion (master), push to docs/** or README.md (master), workflow_dispatch",
          "auth_method": "secrets.SUPERSET_SITE_BUILD (GitHub PAT)",
          "publish_actions": [
            "./.github/actions/github-action-push-to-another-repository"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [],
          "summary": "Builds Superset documentation using Docusaurus (yarn build) and deploys the static site to apache/superset-site repository's asf-site branch (Apache's GitHub Pages hosting). Triggered after Python integration tests complete on master, or by direct pushes to docs paths, or manually. Downloads database diagnostics artifacts from integration test runs to include in docs. Uses a custom GitHub action to push built docs to separate repository.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },

          "file": "superset-extensions-cli.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Superset Extensions CLI Package Tests",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "documentation",
          "ecosystems": [
            "codecov"
          ],
          "trigger": "push to master/release branches and pull_request events",
          "auth_method": "Codecov token (implicit or via GitHub integration)",
          "publish_actions": [

          ],
          "publish_commands": [],
          "summary": "This workflow runs pytest with coverage for the superset-extensions-cli package and uploads coverage reports to Codecov. It also uploads HTML coverage reports to GitHub Actions artifacts (ephemeral storage). The Codecov upload is categorized as documentation publishing.",
          "confidence": "high",

        },
        {
          "file": "superset-helm-release.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Helm: release charts",

          ],

          "publish_actions": [
            "./.github/actions/chart-releaser-action"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [
            "helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"
          ],
          "summary": "Publishes Apache Superset Helm charts to GitHub Pages (gh-pages branch) using chart-releaser-action. Triggered on pushes to master/release branches affecting helm/** files.           "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },

          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "docker_hub"
          ],
          "trigger": "release (published, edited) or workflow_dispatch",

          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "supersetbot docker --push --preset ${{ matrix.build_preset }} --context \"$EVENT\" --context-ref \"$RELEASE\" $FORCE_LATEST --platform \"linux/arm64\" --platform \"linux/amd64\""
          ],
          "summary": "Publishes multi-platform Docker images (linux/arm64, linux/amd64) to Docker Hub for Apache Superset releases. Builds 7 different presets (dev, lean, py310, websocket, dockerize, py311, py312) using a custom supersetbot tool. Triggered on GitHub release events or manually via workflow_dispatch. Also labels PRs with release information.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []

          "detail": "Composite action `setup-backend` directly interpolates `inputs.*` in run block. Not exploitable unless a calling workflow passes attacker-controlled values (PR title, branch name, comment body). Currently a latent injection surface — verify callers only pass trusted input.",

It is manually triggered via workflow_dispatch and uses a force push to replace the production branch content.       "workflows": [
        {
          "file": "build-and-push-images.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Build and push images",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "snapshot_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "ghcr",
            "docker_hub"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_dispatch (manual) and schedule (nightly at 2:00 AM UTC)",
          "auth_method": "GITHUB_TOKEN for GHCR, secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME and secrets.DOCKERHUB_PASSWORD for Docker Hub",
          "publish_actions": [
            "docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9",
            "docker/build-push-action@10e90e3645eae34f1e60eeb005ba3a3d33f178e8"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [
            "docker buildx imagetools create"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow builds and publishes multi-architecture Docker images for Apache Texera services to container registries. It runs on a nightly schedule (2:00 AM UTC) and can be manually triggered. The workflow builds images for multiple services (dashboard, workflow coordinator, workflow runner, access control, config, file, compiling, and computing unit managing services) targeting both AMD64 and ARM64 platforms. Images are pushed to either GHCR (ghcr.io/apache) or Docker Hub with configurable tags. The nightly builds use 'latest' tag and 'main' branch by default. Manual runs allow custom branch, tag, registry, service selection, and platform targeting. This is classified as snapshot_artifact because it publishes nightly builds and development images (using commit hashes or 'latest' tags) rather than versioned releases to public registries.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "create-release-candidate.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Create and upload release candidate artifacts",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "apache_dist"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_dispatch",
          "auth_method": "SVN credentials (secrets.SVN_USERNAME, secrets.SVN_PASSWORD)",
          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "svn commit -m \"Add Apache Texera ${VERSION} RC${RC_NUM} artifacts (source + docker-compose + helm)\" --username \"${{ secrets.SVN_USERNAME }}\" --password \"${{ secrets.SVN_PASSWORD }}\" --no-auth-cache"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow creates Apache Texera release candidate artifacts (source tarball, Docker Compose bundle, Helm chart) from an existing Git tag, signs them with GPG, generates checksums, and publishes them to Apache's SVN distribution repository at dist.apache.org/repos/dist/dev/incubator/texera. This is part of the Apache Software Foundation's release process for incubating projects, where release candidates are staged for community voting before being promoted to the official release repository.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        }
      ],

          "publishes": true,
          "category": "ci_infrastructure",
          "ecosystems": [
            "ghcr"
          ],
          "trigger": "push and pull_request on all branches",
          "auth_method": "GITHUB_TOKEN with docker login to ghcr.io",
          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "docker push \"$($env:DOCKER_IMAGE):$($env:IMAGE_TAG)\""
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow builds and tests Apache Thrift using MSVC in a Windows Docker container. It pushes a Docker image to GHCR (ghcr.io/{owner}/thrift-build) tagged with a hash of the Dockerfile (msvc-{hash}). The image is used as a CI build cache - it's pulled if available, rebuilt if the Dockerfile changes, and pushed back to GHCR only on push events (not PRs). This is infrastructure for CI acceleration, not a release artifact for end users.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "publish.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Publish",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "pypi"
          ],
          "trigger": "release (types: prereleased, released)",
          "auth_method": "password from CloudTruth secrets via env.PYPI_PASSWORD",
          "publish_actions": [
            "./.github/actions/pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [],
          "summary": "Apache Thrift release workflow that publishes Python packages to PyPI. Triggered on GitHub release events (prereleased or released). Uses CloudTruth to retrieve environment-specific credentials and repository URLs - prereleased events publish to test.pypi.org while released events publish to pypi.org. Builds Python sdist and publishes using pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish action.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "pypi.yml",
          "workflow_name": "PyPI publishing",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "pypi"
          ],
          "trigger": "release (types: [published])",
          "auth_method": "OIDC trusted publishing (id-token: write permission)",
          "publish_actions": [
            "pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1"
          ],
          "publish_commands": [
            "python setup.py sdist"
          ],
          "summary": "Publishes Apache Thrift Python package to PyPI when a GitHub release is published. Uses modern OIDC trusted publishing for authentication, builds source distribution with setup.py, and publishes via official pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish action.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []

      "workflows": [
        {
          "file": "distribute-mavencentral-stg.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Distribute from ATR to RAO Maven Staging",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "maven_central"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_dispatch",
          "auth_method": "username_password",
          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "mvn njord:publish -Dnjord.store=$INPUTS_DISTRIBUTION_PACKAGE-00001"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow publishes Maven artifacts to Apache's Repository (RAO) Maven staging repository at repository.apache.org. It is triggered manually via workflow_dispatch, downloads artifacts from Apache Test Release (ATR) system via rsync over SSH, validates them using Maven Njord plugin against Central Requirements, creates a staging tag, and publishes to the staging repository. Authentication uses RAO_USERNAME and RAO_PASSWORD secrets. The workflow integrates with ATR's API to report status throughout the distribution process.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        },
        {
          "file": "distribute-mavencentral.yml",
          "workflow_name": "Distribute from ATR to Maven Central",
          "publishes": true,
          "category": "release_artifact",
          "ecosystems": [
            "maven_central"
          ],
          "trigger": "workflow_dispatch",
          "auth_method": "username_password",
          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "mvn njord:publish -Dnjord.store=$NJORD_STORE -Dnjord.publisher=sonatype-cp -Dnjord.publishingType=automatic"
          ],
          "summary": "This workflow distributes Maven artifacts from Apache's ATR (Apache Test Release) staging system to Maven Central (Sonatype). It authenticates via OIDC JWT to ATR, downloads staged Maven artifacts via rsync over SSH, validates them using the njord-maven-plugin, and publishes to Maven Central with automatic release. The workflow verifies publication status and reports back to ATR throughout the process.",
          "confidence": "high",
          "security_notes": []
        }
      ],

          "summary": "This workflow builds and tests the Apache TrafficServer Ingress Controller, then pushes a Docker image to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io/apache/ats-ingress) tagged as 'latest' on master branch commits. The workflow includes integration tests using Minikube, builds multiple test application images, and performs Trivy security scanning on the published image.",

          "security_notes": []

          "publish_actions": [],
          "publish_commands": [
            "helm package ats-ingress",
            "helm repo index . --url https://apache.github.io/trafficserver-ingress-controller",
            "git push"
          ],
          "summary": "Packages Helm chart using 'helm package', generates repository index with 'helm repo index', commits packaged chart (.tgz) and index.yaml to docs/ directory, and pushes to repository.           "file": ".github/actions/detect-skip-ci/action.yml",
          "detail": "Composite action `detect-skip-ci` directly interpolates `inputs.*` in run block. Not exploitable unless a calling workflow passes attacker-controlled values (PR title, branch name, comment body). Currently a latent injection surface — verify callers only pass trusted input.",
          "line": 48
        }

          "summary": "This workflow builds and tests the Apache XMLSchema project on multiple Java versions (17, 21, 25).
